meta_title: Impact Doors in Fort Lauderdale, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Fort Lauderdale. HVHZ-approved, 170mph rated. Protect your home & save on insurance. Free quotes!
h1: Fort Lauderdale's Trusted Choice for Hurricane-Rated Impact Doors
intro:
Fort Lauderdale is one of South Florida's most iconic cities — a place where million-dollar yachts line the Intracoastal, Las Olas Boulevard pulses with life, and neighborhoods like Rio Vista, Victoria Park, and Las Olas itself boast some of the most architecturally stunning homes in Broward County. But living just one mile from the Atlantic coastline in a High Velocity Hurricane Zone comes with serious responsibility. When a major storm approaches, the doors of your home are among the most vulnerable points of entry — and in Fort Lauderdale, undersized or outdated doors aren't just a liability, they're a code violation waiting to happen.

With an average home value of $480,000 and ZIP codes spanning from the historic streets of Tarpon River (33311) to the upscale waterfront enclaves of Harbor Beach (33316), Fort Lauderdale homeowners have significant investments to protect. Nearly half of all homes in the city were built before 1994 — meaning they predate the sweeping South Florida building code reforms that followed Hurricane Andrew. Many of these homes still have original entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors that were never designed to withstand a 170mph wind event. Whether you live in a mid-century bungalow in Sailboat Bend or a newer townhome in Flagler Village, upgrading to certified impact doors is one of the most impactful safety and financial decisions you can make.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we specialize in the full-service installation of impact-resistant entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors across every Fort Lauderdale neighborhood. Known throughout Broward County as 'The Kings of Service,' our team brings 60+ years of combined installation experience, a 100% in-house workforce, and deep familiarity with the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division's permitting requirements. We don't use subcontractors, we don't cut corners, and we don't leave until the job is done right. When you trust us with your home, you're getting the same level of care and craftsmanship we'd bring to our own families.

section_why.h2: Why Fort Lauderdale Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing for Their Door Installations
section_why.body:
There's no shortage of window and door companies operating in South Florida, but very few can match what Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings to every project in Fort Lauderdale. We've completed over 500 homes across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties, and we carry an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau — not because we talk about quality, but because we deliver it on every single installation. Our reputation was built street by street, neighborhood by neighborhood, from Wilton Manors to Hollywood and everywhere in between.

One of the most important distinctions that sets us apart is our 100% in-house installation model. When you hire Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, you're not being handed off to a rotating roster of subcontractors. Every measurement, every permit application, every installation is performed by our own trained, licensed, and insured crew. This matters enormously in a city like Fort Lauderdale, where HVHZ compliance requires precision workmanship and where the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division conducts thorough inspections. We've learned the local requirements inside and out, and our team is on the job from day one to final inspection sign-off.

Our 60+ years of combined experience means we've seen every type of home Fort Lauderdale has to offer — from older CBS (concrete block structure) homes in Tarpon River and Flagler Village to the luxury waterfront properties lining the canals off Las Olas. We understand how door frames age in Florida's salt air, how humidity affects installation tolerances, and what it takes to get a perfect, weather-tight seal on every unit. Whether you're replacing a single front entry door or retrofitting an entire back wall of sliding glass doors with a panoramic impact system, we have the expertise to handle it without disruption to your daily life.

Financing is available starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options, making it easier than ever for Fort Lauderdale homeowners to upgrade without delay. There's no reason to wait until the next named storm is in the Gulf to take action. We make the process simple, transparent, and affordable — because protecting your home shouldn't be a financial burden.

section_history.h2: Fort Lauderdale's Storm Legacy: Why Impact Doors Are a Non-Negotiable Investment
section_history.body:
Fort Lauderdale sits squarely in one of the most storm-active corridors in the United States. Since 1990, Broward County has been impacted by at least 10 named tropical systems, each leaving behind a trail of property damage that has shaped how residents think about home protection. Hurricane Andrew in 1992 — though it made landfall south of Fort Lauderdale near Homestead — sent a shockwave through the entire region and exposed catastrophic weaknesses in standard residential construction, particularly in doors and windows. The post-Andrew code reforms were sweeping, but they only applied to new construction. Older homes in neighborhoods like Victoria Park and Sailboat Bend were left with their pre-code vulnerabilities intact.

Hurricane Wilma in 2005 was Fort Lauderdale's most direct major hit in recent memory. Wilma crossed the peninsula as a Category 3 storm and delivered sustained winds well above 100mph across Broward County, destroying roofs, shattering unprotected doors and windows, and flooding homes from Miami-Dade to Palm Beach. The storm exposed just how vulnerable the region's older housing stock remained, even after a decade of code reform. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, which tracked directly up the spine of Florida and delivered tropical storm to Category 1 force winds to Fort Lauderdale, causing widespread power outages and significant property damage. Irma was a reminder that no storm season should be taken lightly — especially for homeowners living within a mile of the Atlantic.

Impact doors are the front-line defense against these events. Unlike traditional doors secured by a single latch point, modern impact doors feature multi-point locking systems and laminated glass or panel cores engineered to absorb and deflect energy from wind-borne debris traveling at extreme speeds. In a storm like Wilma, the difference between an impact door and a standard door can mean the difference between a dry, intact home and a catastrophic breach that allows internal pressure to destroy your roof structure from the inside out.

section_code.h2: HVHZ Compliance and Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Fort Lauderdale
section_code.body:
Fort Lauderdale is located entirely within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone — the most stringent residential construction classification in the United States. The HVHZ designation, which covers all of Broward and Miami-Dade counties, was created specifically in response to the catastrophic failures exposed by Hurricane Andrew. Under this classification, all exterior doors, windows, and glazed openings must meet Florida Building Code Chapter 16 (High Velocity Hurricane Zone) requirements, which mandate impact resistance testing standards far exceeding those required in the rest of Florida. Any door installed in a Fort Lauderdale home must be tested and approved for the HVHZ, and that approval must be documented as part of the permitting process.

For Fort Lauderdale specifically, wind design pressures are calculated for a 170mph design wind speed — one of the highest in the continental U.S. This means every impact door installed in the city must carry a valid Notice of Acceptance (NOA) from Miami-Dade County Product Control, which is the recognized testing authority for HVHZ-rated products. The NOA must correspond to the specific product model, size, and installation configuration used in your project. Choosing a door that doesn't carry the correct NOA — or installing it incorrectly — will result in a failed inspection and potential fines from the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division.

All door replacement projects in Fort Lauderdale require a permit issued by the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division, located at City Hall. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permitting process on your behalf. We prepare and submit all required documentation, coordinate inspections, and ensure that every installation meets or exceeds both the Florida Building Code and local amendments enforced by the city. We also ensure all work is completed by licensed contractors, so your homeowner's insurance remains valid and your investment is fully protected under warranty.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Premium HVHZ-Approved Products for Fort Lauderdale Homes
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two of the industry's most respected impact door lines in Fort Lauderdale: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are specifically engineered for South Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone conditions and carry Miami-Dade NOA certifications that satisfy Fort Lauderdale's stringent permitting requirements. Whether you're looking for a bold, statement-making entry door for your Las Olas home or a seamless panoramic sliding glass door system opening to your Rio Vista backyard, we carry the configurations, sizes, and finishes to make it happen.

ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) produces impact entry doors, French doors, and sliding glass doors built to withstand the punishing coastal conditions that Fort Lauderdale homeowners know all too well. ESW doors feature laminated safety glass with a durable interlayer that holds the pane together even when impacted by high-speed debris — preventing the dangerous breach that can trigger internal pressure collapse in a storm. The door frames are engineered from high-strength aluminum or fiberglass, offering excellent resistance to corrosion in Fort Lauderdale's salt-air environment. Multi-point locking systems are standard across the ESW lineup, providing structural integrity at multiple points along the door frame rather than relying on a single latch mechanism.

CWI rounds out our product offering with an equally impressive lineup of HVHZ-certified impact doors designed for both performance and aesthetics. CWI's sliding glass door systems are particularly well-suited for Fort Lauderdale's open-concept waterfront and canal homes, delivering expansive glass panels with clean sightlines and smooth operation. Their entry door collection offers customizable panel designs, glass insert options, and hardware finishes that complement everything from a traditional Key West-style cottage in Tarpon River to a contemporary new-build in Flagler Village. Both ESW and CWI products come backed by manufacturer warranties and are installed by our in-house team to exacting HVHZ standards.

section_cost.h2: What Fort Lauderdale Homeowners Can Expect to Pay for Impact Door Installation
section_cost.body:
With an average home value of $480,000 in Fort Lauderdale, impact door upgrades represent a smart, high-return investment in your property's safety, comfort, and market appeal. The cost of impact door installation varies depending on the type, size, and number of doors being replaced, as well as any structural modifications required to the opening. A single impact entry door installation typically ranges from $1,500 to $4,500 installed, while sliding glass door systems — particularly larger multi-panel configurations common in Fort Lauderdale's waterfront and pool homes — can range from $3,500 to $10,000 or more depending on width and configuration. French door systems fall in a similar range. Keep in mind that in the HVHZ, product and installation standards are higher, and pricing reflects the certified workmanship and code-compliant products required.

For homeowners who want to act now without draining savings or disrupting monthly cash flow, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available. Our financing programs are designed to make HVHZ-compliant upgrades accessible to Fort Lauderdale homeowners at every budget level. Spreading the cost over time means you can protect your home before hurricane season — not after a damage event forces your hand at a much greater expense. We work with multiple lending partners to find terms that fit your situation.

From a return-on-investment standpoint, impact doors in Fort Lauderdale deliver on multiple fronts. In addition to the insurance savings detailed in the following section, impact doors increase resale value, reduce energy bills by improving thermal efficiency and reducing solar heat gain, and virtually eliminate noise infiltration from Fort Lauderdale's busy streets and water traffic. Real estate agents across Broward County consistently note that impact-protected homes command a premium over comparable unprotected properties — making this upgrade one of the few home improvements that pays dividends before, during, and after a storm.

section_insurance.h2: Unlock Significant Insurance Savings with Impact Doors in Broward County
section_insurance.body:
One of the most financially compelling reasons to install impact doors in Fort Lauderdale is the substantial reduction in homeowner's insurance premiums that certified impact-resistant openings can deliver. Florida's insurance market has become increasingly challenging — and expensive — in recent years, with many major national carriers pulling out of the state entirely. Broward County homeowners have felt this acutely, with wind coverage premiums rising sharply year over year. Impact doors, combined with impact windows, can qualify your home for wind mitigation credits under Florida Statute 627.0629, which requires insurance companies to offer discounts for verified storm-resistant features.

To access these discounts, you'll need a Wind Mitigation Inspection Report completed by a licensed Florida inspector. The inspection evaluates your home's opening protections among other structural features and assigns ratings that your insurer uses to calculate your premium reduction. Homes with fully protected openings — meaning all windows and doors are impact-rated — typically receive the highest tier of discount. For Fort Lauderdale homeowners paying annual wind premiums of $3,000 to $8,000 or more (which is common in Broward County coastal ZIP codes like 33304, 33305, and 33316), a 25% to 45% reduction in wind coverage costs can translate to thousands of dollars in annual savings. Many homeowners recoup the cost of their impact door installation within just a few years through insurance savings alone.

Common carriers writing policies in Broward County — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Property & Casualty, and others — all recognize and reward certified impact door and window installations. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing provides you with all manufacturer product certifications, NOA documents, and installation permits needed to submit to your insurance carrier for a wind mitigation re-rating. We've helped hundreds of Fort Lauderdale homeowners navigate this process and secure the savings they deserve. The combination of storm protection, energy efficiency, and insurance premium reduction makes impact door installation one of the highest-ROI upgrades available to South Florida homeowners today.

faqs.q: Do I really need HVHZ-rated impact doors in Fort Lauderdale, or will standard impact doors work?
faqs.a: Fort Lauderdale falls entirely within Florida's High Velocity Hurricane Zone, which means only doors carrying a valid Miami-Dade Notice of Acceptance (NOA) for HVHZ use are legally permitted for installation. Standard impact-rated doors approved for other parts of Florida do not meet Fort Lauderdale's building code requirements. All ESW and CWI products we install carry the appropriate HVHZ certifications and will pass City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division inspections.

faqs.q: How much do impact doors cost in Fort Lauderdale, and what financing options are available?
faqs.a: Impact door installation in Fort Lauderdale typically ranges from $1,500 to $4,500 for a single entry door and $3,500 to $10,000+ for sliding glass or French door systems, depending on size and configuration. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at just $87 per month with $0 down options available. Our team will provide a detailed, transparent quote based on your specific home and openings — no surprises, no hidden fees.

faqs.q: Do I need a permit to replace my doors with impact doors in Fort Lauderdale?
faqs.a: Yes. All exterior door replacements in Fort Lauderdale require a permit issued by the City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division. The permit package must include proof that the selected product holds a valid Miami-Dade NOA for HVHZ use and that installation will be performed by a licensed contractor. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process on your behalf, from application submission to final inspection, ensuring full code compliance throughout.

faqs.q: How long does impact door installation take in Fort Lauderdale?
faqs.a: Most residential impact door installations in Fort Lauderdale are completed in one to two days, depending on the number of openings and any required frame modifications. The permitting process typically takes one to three weeks prior to installation, depending on current City of Fort Lauderdale Building Division workload. We'll coordinate everything in advance so your installation day runs smoothly, and we handle the final inspection scheduling as well.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors actually lower my homeowner's insurance premium in Broward County?
faqs.a: Yes — in many cases, significantly. Certified impact doors and windows qualify for wind mitigation credits under Florida law, which can reduce your wind coverage premium by 25% to 45% or more. For Fort Lauderdale homeowners in coastal ZIP codes paying high wind premiums, this can mean $1,000 to $3,000+ in annual savings. A licensed wind mitigation inspector will document your upgrades, and we provide all necessary product certifications and permit records to support your re-rating request to your insurer.
